sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.84 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: Yu.\,I.\,Merzlyakov (Sov. Math. Dokl., 19 (1978), 64--68) proved that if the complex numbers$, ,$are each at least 3 in absolute value, then the matrices {$( {cc}1& \\0&1 )$}, \ {$( {cc}1&0 \\ &1 )$}, \ and \ {$( {cc}1- &- \\ &1+ )$} generate a free group of rank three. Are there rational numbers$, ,$, each less than 3 in absolute value, with the same property? }

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
